name: Giants in History
description: The concept of 'Giants in History' encompasses claims that beings of extraordinary size existed in the human past, influencing ancient civilizations or leaving behind physical evidence of their presence. These claims often draw upon interpretations of ancient myths, legends, and religious texts, such as the Nephilim in the Bible, stories of Titans in Greek mythology, and various giant figures in folklore from around the world. Proponents point to archaeological discoveries of unusually large skeletal remains, megalithic structures allegedly too large for modern humans to build, and anomalous footprints as evidence. Mainstream archaeology and anthropology generally attribute such findings to misidentification of animal bones, natural geological formations, exaggerated storytelling, or the skillful engineering of ancient cultures with advanced (but human-scale) technologies. Skepticism also addresses the lack of consistent, verifiable evidence of widespread giant populations and the biological implausibility of maintaining such large beings without significant skeletal and organ adaptations not found in the purported remains. The interpretation of ancient texts as literal accounts of giants is also challenged, suggesting symbolic or metaphorical meanings instead. The theory often intersects with alternative history, lost civilization theories, and pseudoscientific interpretations of ancient cultures.
subtitle: Ancient Legends and Archaeological Anomalies

claims.claim: Giant skeletons have been discovered, proving their existence.
claims.evidence: Reports of large skeletal remains in newspaper articles, historical accounts, and some archaeological digs.
claims.criticism: Most reports are unsubstantiated, misidentified animal bones, or hoaxes. Reputable archaeological finds of unusually large individuals are rare and fall within the range of human variation, albeit at the extreme end.

claims.claim: Megalithic structures were built by giants due to their immense size and weight.
claims.evidence: Structures like Stonehenge, the Pyramids of Giza, and Baalbek's Trilithon are too massive for regular humans to have constructed.
claims.criticism: Mainstream archaeology demonstrates that ancient cultures possessed advanced engineering techniques, manpower, and organizational skills capable of moving and erecting large stones. No evidence links these structures to beings of abnormal size.

claims.claim: Ancient texts and myths describe encounters with giants.
claims.evidence: References to Nephilim in the Bible, Titans in Greek mythology, and similar giant figures in various cultures.
claims.criticism: These texts are primarily mythological and symbolic, not literal historical accounts. Interpretations as literal descriptions of giant races are often based on selective readings and disregard for literary context.

keyFigures.name: Zecharia Sitchin
keyFigures.role: Popularized the idea of ancient astronauts and giants in his interpretations of Sumerian texts.

keyFigures.name: Chris Stringer
keyFigures.role: Paleoanthropologist who has critiqued claims of giant skeletons and promotes mainstream understanding of human evolution.

timeline.event: Reports of giant skeleton discoveries become common in newspapers and popular literature.
Zecharia Sitchin's books popularize the idea of ancient giants interacting with humanity.
Continued debate and investigation of archaeological anomalies and ancient texts in relation to giant claims.

sources.author: Childress, David Hatcher
sources.work: Technology of the Gods: The Incredible Sciences of the Ancients
sources.title: Childress, David Hatcher

sources.author: Feder, Kenneth L.
sources.work: Frauds, Myths, and Mysteries: Science and Pseudoscience in Archaeology
sources.title: Feder, Kenneth L.
